elements.name: Title
texts: "They come to Lough Derg to fast and pray and beg"

elements.name: Description
texts: The poetry of Patrick Kavanagh's 'Lough Derg', detailing a visit in 1941

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts:
“From Cavan and from Leitrim and from Mayo, 
From all the thin-faced parishes where hills
Are perished noses running peaty water,
They come to Lough Derg to fast and pray and beg
With all the bitterness of nonentities, and the envy
Of the inarticulate when dealing with the artist.”
